Querying recipes.title: PostgreSQL LEFT JOIN or LEFT OUTER JOIN - w3resource
Querying recipes.description: The PostgreSQL LEFT JOIN, joins two tables and fetches rows based on a condition, which are matching in both the tables, and the unmatched rows will also be available from the table written before the JOIN clause.

Querying recipes.description: I have a datetime field in my Postgresql, named "dt". I'd like to do something like SELECT * FROM myTable WHERE extract (date from dt) = '01/01/11' What is the right syntax to do that? Thanks!
